2026-03-26T03:51:11.9695762Z - **No alpha versions** anywhere.
2026-03-26T03:51:11.9698453Z - **No naming violations** (`_N` suffixes, `AutoGenerated` suffixes, `_` prefixes) in the public API.
2026-03-26T03:51:11.9699702Z - **Type safety**: `unknown` only appears in `ErrorAdditionalInfo.info` (acceptable per guidelines); `any` only in the standard `restorePoller` signature.
2026-03-26T03:51:11.9700551Z - **No `void` misuse**: only on `delete` operations (appropriate).

2026-03-26T03:51:35.1420979Z Agent:    - CHANGELOG is appropriate for an initial release with no prior version to compare against.
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1421871Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1422238Z Agent: 4. **API Design Issues**:
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1423614Z Agent:    - `AzureClouds` enum uses `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` members (`AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`), which I should verify against the SDK naming conventions.
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1427022Z Agent:    - `AzureSupportedClouds` is defined as a template literal type based on the enum, creating a closed type that doesn't allow extensibility. I'm reconsidering whether this aligns with SDK guidelines that prefer `string` types for extensible enums, though this particular pattern might be acceptable since it's directly tied to the enum.
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1429550Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1431477Z Agent:    Looking at the enum members themselves, `AzureClouds` uses UPPER_SNAKE_CASE which violates the PascalCase convention for enum members. This is a recurring pattern I've seen flagged before in similar packages like arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensiontypes and arm-applink.
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1434031Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1437824Z Agent:    For the `autoPublish: false` setting on this new package, I need to check whether that's intentional for beta releases or if it's an oversight. Let me review what the management review guidelines actually specify for these cases. I'm noticing the `AzureClouds` enum uses UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for its members when PascalCase would be more consistent with TypeScript conventions. The `KnownVersions` enum follows the standard pattern with its `V` prefix and camelCase formatting, and while `AppLinkClien... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2557 chars]

2026-03-26T03:51:35.1460792Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1465402Z Agent: 4. **AzureClouds enum UPPER_SNAKE_CASE** - Looking back at the naming validation rules, they focus on avoiding `_N` suffixes, `AutoGenerated` suffixes, `_` prefixes, and duplicate prefixes on models. The guidelines don't explicitly call out UPPER_SNAKE_CASE enum members as a violation, so this appears to be acceptable. I'm checking past PR reviews to see if UPPER_SNAKE_CASE naming for enum members was previously flagged as a design issue, and now I need to verify whether the guidelines actually prohibit this pattern. The guidelines explicitly call out `_N` suffixes, `AutoGenerated` suffixes, and `_` prefixes as violations, but UPPER_SNAKE_CASE itself isn't directly mentioned, so I should be cautious about flagging it without explicit guideline support.
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1469804Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:51:35.1471412Z Agent: Now I'm systematically reviewing the API surface for other potential issues—checking type safety in return types like the delete operation's `PollerLike<OperationState<void>, void>`, verifying there are no problematic `unknown` types that users would need to cast, and confirming this is a new package with no breaking changes to worry about.

I'm also examining whether `AzureSupportedClouds` being a closed union type rather than an open string aligns with the guidelines, and since it's based on an enum, it's appropriately constrained.

This will vary based on the \"method\" provided, so ensure you provide the correct ID:\n- Do not provide any resource ID for 'list_workflows' method.\n- Provide a workflow ID or workflow file name (e.g. ci.yaml) for 'list_workflow_runs' method, or omit to list all workflow runs in the repository.\n- Provide a workflow run ID for 'list_workflow_jobs' and 'list_workflow_run_artifacts' methods.\n","type":"string"},"workflow_jobs_filter":{"description":"Filters for workflow jobs. **ONLY** used when method is 'list_workflow_jobs'","properties":{"filter":{"description":"Filters jobs by their completed_at timestamp","enum":["latest","all"],"type":"string"}},"type":"object"},"workflow_runs_filter":{"description":"Filters for workflow runs. **ONLY** used when method is 'list_workflow_runs'","properties":{"actor":{"description":"Filter to a specific GitHub user's workflow runs.","type":"string"},"branch":{"description":"Filter workflow runs to a specific Git branch. Default is true.","type":"boolean"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"sha":{"description":"Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","sha"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_commit","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"dark"}]},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get file or directory contents"},"description":"Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository","inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization)","type":"string"},"path":{"default":"/","description":"Path to file/directory","type":"string"},"ref":{"description":"Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"sha":{"description":"Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_file_contents","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABgAAAAYCAYAAADgdz34AAAABmJLR0QA/wD/AP+gvaeTAAAA+ElEQVRIie2WLU5DQRRGzwUEirQCQ9BsoSGtZQHgSFgIsg4JXQCGP8V2QCBIEATZkJDgOJi+MJnMo30TFHmfmzvzfeeKyZ2BROpQvVHfrddDmhkZ4BY4Ai6BD7prAowjIoq7i85nFcGNf6qa1tayM1vAvBZQUg74c/WAHtAD/gMgn6YCT8...

The Azure AppLink client can use Azure Active Directory credentials to authenticate.\\n+You can find the endpoint for your Azure AppLink resource in the [Azure Portal][azure_portal].\\n+\\n+You can authenticate with Azure Active Directory using a credential from the [@azure/identity][azure_identity] library or [an existing AAD Token](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/sdk/identity/identity/samples/AzureIdentityExamples.md#authenticating-with-a-pre-fetched-access-token).\\n+\\n+To use the [DefaultAzureCredential][defaultazurecredential] provider shown below, or other credential providers provided with the Azure SDK, please install the `@azure/identity` package:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+npm install @azure/identity\\n+```\\n+\\n+You will also need to **register a new AAD application and grant access to Azure AppLink** by assigning the suitable role to your service principal (note: roles such as `\\\"Owner\\\"` will not grant the necessary permissions).\\n+\\n+For more information about how to create an Azure AD Application check out [this guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/howto-create-service-principal-portal).\\n+\\n+Using Node.js and Node-like environments, you can use the `DefaultAzureCredential` class to authenticate the client.\\n+\\n+```ts snippet:ReadmeSampleCreateClient_Node\\n+import { AppLinkClient } from \\\"@azure/arm-appnetwork\\\";\\n+import { DefaultAzureCredential } from \\\"@azure/identity\\\";\\n+\\n+const subscriptionId = \\\"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\\";\\
